item.description: StatMuse: Fun Fact: Dylan Harper is undefeated as a starter.

item.title: At 20 yr-77 days, he also became the youngest player …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: Dylan Harper broke the Spurs’ record for most steals in a playoff game with 7 (Kawhi had 6). At 20 yr-77 days, he also became the youngest player in NBA history to record 20+ points and 10+ rebounds in a conference finals game (beating Magic Johnson, at 20 yr-260). (via @statitudes.com ) #NBA

item.title: "That was the hardest game I ever played in my life!" …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: "That was the hardest game I ever played in my life!" — Stephon Castle after Game 1 (via @nba.com ) #NBA

item.title: Magic Johnson: I hate to break the news to the rest of …
item.description: Magic Johnson: I hate to break the news to the rest of the Western Conference, but they may not have a chance to win the Western Conference Finals for the next 5-7 years. The Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs are just that good! They are talented, deep, athletic and both teams are well coached.

item.title: Rival teams don't view AJ Dybantsa as a lock to go first
item.description: Rival teams don't view Dybantsa as a lock to go first, however, as there has been no true consensus No. 1 among scouts all season. Darryn Peterson, Cam Boozer and Caleb Wilson are all expected to receive consideration as the Wizards go through their process. The fact that there is a consensus group of four has led some executives to view the top of this draft as more ripe for trades than in a typical year, but only if the top four teams see value in moving around within that tier. Any trade dyna

item.title: The network will begin broadcasting the series between …
item.description: ESPN’s plans for Inside the NBA for the Eastern Conference Finals have come into focus. The network will begin broadcasting the series between the Knicks and Cavaliers on Tuesday night. An ESPN spokesperson confirmed to Front Office Sports that the plans for the iconic studio show, which it is licensing from TNT, are as follows: Every ECF game will have a one-hour pregame show, except for Game 3 on Sat. May 23, which is on ABC and will have a half-hour pregame. Halftime of every game Postgame sh

item.title: The world is 8 billion people, so it’s 8 billion …
item.description: Jared Weiss: Asked Victor Wembanyama if he feels he is the best basketball player in the world now: “Do I feel like it right now? I feel tired. But it’s not a question I’m wondering right now. We’ll see. The world is 8 billion people, so it’s 8 billion opinions.”

item.title: "I knew, deep down, I had that itch to play," Ingles …
item.description: That's the prevailing reason Joe Ingles - now 38 - has signed a two-year deal with Melbourne United, which the team officially announced on Tuesday, returning to Australia's National Basketball League 17 years after leaving the league in 2009 following a three-year stint with the South Dragons. "I knew, deep down, I had that itch to play," Ingles told ESPN. "We would play pick-up every game day, and I would be competing, I would play really well, I felt really good. I obviously just wasn't getti

item.title: "I wouldn't have done this if I didn't believe that," …
item.description: "I wouldn't have done this if I didn't believe that," Ingles said of being able to make a meaningful impact in the NBL. "I understand where I'm at in my career; I'm not 21 anymore. I'm very self-aware of where I'm at in my career. If I didn't truly believe that I could come in and make an impact and help this team win games, I wouldn't have done it. I'm not doing it for financial needs, I'm not doing it for the notoriety of coming back. I've played 20 years professionally, I've played 12 in the

item.title: Peterson came across as quiet but serious in …
item.description: NBA teams are still awaiting the results of Darryn Peterson's medical evaluation, which is viewed around the league as the most important off-court piece after he dealt with serious cramping concerns, which he attributed to high dosages of creatine. According to team sources, Peterson came across as quiet but serious in interviews last week and handled that part of the process well, addressing questions about his strange season. Where that leaves him on draft night will play out in the coming we

item.title: Oklahoma City heads to the municipal market this week …
item.description: Oklahoma City heads to the municipal market this week with a $762.4 million revenue bond deal that will finance most of the cost of a new city-owned home for the National Basketball Association's Thunder. The bonds, which are structured with serial maturities between 2029 and 2034 and will be issued through the Oklahoma City Public Property Authority, are backed by city sales and use tax revenue projected to total $1.18 billion, assuming 1.68% annual growth in sales tax revenue and no growth in

item.title: Billy Donovan has an 'excellent shot' of landing Magic head coaching job
item.description: While I always hesitate to frame any potential hire as the favorite for a job, since NBA matters can be so fluid, coaching insiders across the league's landscape are very much operating under the belief that former Bulls coach Billy Donovan has an excellent shot at landing the post. Sources confirmed that Donovan is in dialogue with Magic president Jeff Weltman and will have an in-person meeting with Orlando's lead executive in the near future…Orlando has LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gun

item.title: "It's obviously challenging -- very tall, very long, …
item.description: Wembanyama blocked three shots, including a midrange step-back jumper by Gilgeous-Alexander on the baseline midway through the fourth quarter, but that total hardly shows his defensive impact. His presence eliminated driving lanes and intimidated the Thunder's off-dribble creators, even the two-time MVP. "It's obviously challenging -- very tall, very long, deters a lot of things at the rim," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "You've got to be smart when you go in there. Be patient, but also be aggressive

item.title: "I just felt like it was what the game needed -- to …
item.description: Gilgeous-Alexander slithered through a crack in the defense for a dunk over Wembanyama to give the Thunder a three-point lead with 58 seconds remaining in the first overtime. But Wembanyama answered with a deep 3, and Gilgeous-Alexander didn't have another bucket in either of the overtime periods. "I just felt like it was what the game needed -- to try to be aggressive, touch the paint, make plays," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "Didn't make enough of them tonight personally, but I think that's what

item.title: "We're getting past the point of potential... that's …
item.description: Run It Back: DeMarcus Cousins says Victor Wembanyama is the real deal 💯 "We're getting past the point of potential... that's scary because he still has a lot of growing to do. ... Everything you want in a young franchise player, he's the perfect example of it."

item.title: "I put this [Game 1 loss] on Chet [Holmgren]. You have …
item.description: Clutch Points: "I put this [Game 1 loss] on Chet [Holmgren]. You have to step up and accept that challenge... he tucked his tail." DeMarcus Cousins on the Thunder big man getting outplayed by Victor Wembanyama 👀

item.title: Last night, 22-year-old Victor Wembanyama became the …
item.description: Todd Whitehead: Last night, 22-year-old Victor Wembanyama became the youngest of 14 players in NBA history to score 40+ points with 20+ rebounds in a playoff game.

item.title: Pistons president on Jalen Duren: We look forward to getting a deal done and for him to continue being a Piston
item.description: Michael Scotto: Detroit Pistons President Trajan Langdon on Jalen Duren: “JD had a fantastic season. All-Star. One of the biggest contributors to us being a No. 1 seed… We look forward to coming together with his representation, getting a deal done, and for him continuing to be a Piston.”

item.title: Pistons hope to re-sign Tobias Harris: His professionalism has been huge for us
item.description: Michael Scotto: Trajan Langdon on Tobias Harris: “His approach and professionalism has been huge in terms of us establishing the environment we have in the past two years… They call him Unc for a reason… We hope that we can bring him back. He’s somebody we want to keep in a Pistons uniform.”

item.title: The Nets shocked a lot of people when they took Egor …
item.description: The Nets shocked a lot of people when they took Egor Dёmin with the eighth pick one year ago. And maybe they will shock again this year. Multiple league sources have connected Brooklyn to Michigan center Aday Mara and Tennessee freshman forward Nate Ament. But in this mock, we’re sticking with the best guard available.

item.title: Sources around the league widely believe the Kings are …
item.description: Sources around the league widely believe the Kings are targeting Acuff with this pick. There is a lot connecting them. Kings general manager Scott Perry and assistant general manager B.J. Armstrong are both born and raised in Detroit. So is Acuff. Perry coached Acuff’s father at Eastern Kentucky in the late 1990s. Maybe it really is that simple. There’s also the obvious fact that he fits Sacramento’s needs.

item.title: Bulls, Magic and Trail Blazers are all slated to make …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bulls, Magic and Trail Blazers are all slated to make formal interview requests for all three franchises’ respective coaching vacancies this week, sources say.

item.title: The exec said: “Luka Doncic is 27, he just turned 27. …
item.description: As one Western Conference executive said of the Lakers, the plan is to build around Luka Doncic as the start with Austin Reaves (also a potential free agent this summer) as the franchise focal points. They could bring back Giannis Antetokounmpo, but other than an exception for him, the Lakers want to get younger and better suited to a Doncic-Reaves core. The exec said: “Luka Doncic is 27, he just turned 27. Austin Reaves is 27. That’s what they’re looking for, guy in their early prime who can gr

item.title: “Definitely a big summer. There is going to be a lot …
item.description: Whatever happens with the Lakers, and with Antetokounmpo, they will be part of what is certain to be a critical summer in the NBA, no matter the direction each takes. Said the exec: “Definitely a big summer. There is going to be a lot of movement this summer, there are going to be a lot of trades, before the draft, after the draft in July, going into August and September. There are a lot of teams that see themselves in need of a change, so you will see some bad contracts getting moved around wit

item.title: Coach search will be heating up next. On our By The …
item.description: KC Johnson: Officially official Coach search will be heating up next. On our By The Horns podcast, we’ve been consistent to watch for some of these names: Wes Unseld Jr., James Borrego, Sean Sweeney and Micah Nori. According to @JakeLFischer , OKC’s Dave Bliss could be in mix as well.

item.description: Steve Popper: Pepsi planting Knicks playoff tickets around NY.

item.title: Jarrett Allen on 'lights were too bright' comment from 2023: I'm a new person now, I've grown and evolved
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “That comment was that comment. What I said is what it is. I’m a new person now. I’ve grown, evolved, have a lot more playoff series under my belt, a lot more games under my belt …” - Jarrett Allen on his “lights are brighter than expected” at MSG quote during the 2023 playoffs

item.title: Sources say Tom Thibodeau, to the contrary, is not …
item.description: Sources say Tom Thibodeau, to the contrary, is not involved in the Magic's interview process at this point. Yet it's well-established that Orlando wants to hire a coach with experience and sources tell both Marc Stein and me that Orlando has LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy on its radar as well. The defensive guru on Tyronn Lue's staff is the brother, of course, of former Magic coach Stan Van Gundy.

item.title: In-demand Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney is …
item.description: In-demand Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney is another candidate expected to interview with the Magic, sources say, even though Sweeney is seeking his first head coaching post. Sweeney's first formal conversation with Orlando might have to be conducted remotely given Sweeney's Western Conference finals duties with San Antonio. League sources say Sweeney is also a prime candidate for Chicago's opening created by Donovan's departure.

item.title: I've also been advised that Chicago is likely to …
item.description: I've also been advised that Chicago is likely to formally request to interview Tiago Splitter, who is still under contract with Portland for next season even as the Trail Blazers actively speak to external candidates about their coaching job. Splitter went 42-39 as Portland's interim coach this season and steered the team to its first playoff berth in five years after Chauncey Billups was placed on indefinite leave due to a federal gambling probe just one game into the season.

item.title: That's the same ballpark we're expecting to see for …
item.description: That's the same ballpark we're expecting to see for what sources say is a looming extension in Atlanta for Quin Snyder as well. It's not just Mike Brown in New York commanding those kinds of figures; that has become the going rate for proven head coaches.

item.title: “This is a business trip. This isn’t something where …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: “This is a business trip. This isn’t something where I’m here to be like, ‘Man, I’m so excited to be home.’ I did that already. And we lost.” - Westchester County, NY native Donovan Mitchell on the Cavs’ second playoff series versus his hometown Knicks in the last four years

item.title: "JD had a fantastic season. All-Star, one of the …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: On Duren, Langdon sounds confident they'll extend him in some capacity. "JD had a fantastic season. All-Star, one of the biggest contributors to us getting a one seed … an incredible leap from last year to this year because of the work he put in last summer and his focus."

item.title: "I think defenses took some things away from him and …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon on Duren's postseason: "I think defenses took some things away from him and that's the biggest thing ... I think he would say I didn’t play the basketball I would’ve liked to have played. Sometimes that happens in the league with young players."

item.title: "I don’t think there was a rock that was not …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon of if they were "seduced" by their regular season and didn't do enough at the trade deadline: "I don’t think there was a rock that was not overturned as far as understanding where we were ... some things were way too expensive at that point, we felt."

item.title: Says Ausar spent a lot of time last summer on his …
item.description: Ku: Trajan Langdon says Ausar Thompson was "big time" and he had an incredible season. Says that he impacts the game in both seasons. Says Ausar spent a lot of time last summer on his offensive game, but had restrictions all summer. Says this summer will not have any restrictions, he will work even more on his offensive game, and he will be even better next year.

item.title: “The league is in trouble for a very long time.”
item.description: Jared Weiss: Devin Vassell on the Spurs’ young talent: “The league is in trouble for a very long time.”

item.title: "We're just super connected on the court when it gets …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "We're just super connected on the court when it gets loud like that. You can't really hear fairly often but you know each other, you know what the next person is doing, what plays they're supposed to be running." -Vassell on executing in the road environment

item.title: "He'll do everything he can (to play), which is mostly …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson indicated Fox (sore right ankle) will be a game-time decision Wednesday night. "He'll do everything he can (to play), which is mostly rest and then get treatment to prepare for the next game, and tomorrow pregame we'll find out if he can," the #Spurs coach said.

item.title: Was told around 70 people were let go today in the …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Was told around 70 people were let go today in the Blazers' layoffs on the business side. Know some of the names but out of respect to them I'll let them announce it when and how they will.

item.title: Timberwolves optimistic they will re-sign Ayo Dosunmu
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Tim Connelly says he is optimistic they will bring back Ayo Dosunmu: "Ayo’s our most important free agent. He’s a guy we thought we knew pretty well and we liked him from afar. Now seeing him day to day, we love him."

item.title: “Everything is on the table. You can’t just throw one …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Mark Daigneault on what he saw when Chet Holmgren defended Victor Wembanyama during Game 1: “Everything is on the table. You can’t just throw one pitch throughout the whole series. We have reasons why we approached the game the way we did last night. Some of that was the regular-season matchups. Some of that was the stuff we saw in the first couple of rounds from ourselves and from them. But in these series, one of the things you’ve got to do is you’ve got to figure out what the

item.title: “A combination of things. I agree with that …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Mark Daigneault on what OKC did to keep San Antonio out of the paint early on in Game 1 and how it can replicate that for a full game tomorrow: “A combination of things. I agree with that assessment. When you’re looking at a game like that, you’re trying to show what’s sustainable for you and the opponent. Certainly, Wembanyama getting around the basket and being able to dunk the ball or us having to foul out of weakness to prevent a layup isn’t sustainable. The more you give th

item.title: “The good news is Shai has been this player for a long …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mark Daigneault, asked how to get SGA more offensive cracks: “The good news is Shai has been this player for a long time, and we've seen everything, including this. We have our attacks that we have to use against that, and I thought last night when we did those well, we got pretty good cracks.”

item.title: “He’s a competitor. He works really hard, and he’s …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Asked Lu Dort what makes him feel Chet Holmgren can bounce back after an underwhelming performance in Game 1: “He’s a competitor. He works really hard, and he’s human. Not everybody is going to have their best game every time. We know what he’s capable of. We know what he can do on the court, so we all expect Chet to have a better game moving forward. It’s not just him. A lot of guys didn’t have their best game as well, but we all know Chet. We all know he’s going to bounce back

item.title: "I thought Cade was an absolute warrior. I haven’t had …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon: "I thought Cade was an absolute warrior. I haven’t had a collapsed lung, I’m sure most of you haven’t, and he had a collapsed lung and fractured rib." Noted he had to sit on the couch for 7-10 days to heal and went through deconditioning right as they ramped up for playoffs.

item.title: "I know it frustrated him a lot because he didn’t get …
item.description: Omari Sanokfa II: Langdon said Cade will learn lot from the playoffs, and his lung injury: "I know it frustrated him a lot because he didn’t get to be his best self. Nobody can in that situation. He’ll learn from it. He’s probably going to look at everything and say I have to be more efficient."

item.title: "Did I think it would equal 60 wins? Even after the …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon said he knew the team could be good after the 13-game winning streak: "Did I think it would equal 60 wins? Even after the winning streak I didn’t think it would be 60 wins and that’s an incredible feat, one that hadn't been done here in nearly 20 years."

item.title: "He’s a better player now than when we got him off …
item.description: Omari Sanokfa II: Langdon on Paul Reed: "Consummate professional. In and out of the lineup this season and it didn’t matter if his number was called." "He’s a better player now than when we got him off waivers two years ago."

item.title: Trajan says he believes Ron Holland losing his spot in …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Trajan says he believes Ron Holland losing his spot in the rotation will motivate him over the summer and thinks Ron will improve next season because of that frustration. Noted how helpful Ron was to the Pistons winning 60 games during the regular season.

item.title: “He was masterful in the Denver series. We’re not here …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Wrote the day after the season ended that Chris Finch would be back. Tim Connelly today: “He was masterful in the Denver series. We’re not here w/o Finchy, the playoff success he’s had. I’m just thankful he’s a partner and thankful he’s our head coach.”

item.title: Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr., who started all 40 …
item.description: Jeff Borzello: BREAKING: Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr., who started all 40 games en route to a national championship, is keeping his name in the NBA draft, his agents Austin Brown, Aaron Mintz and Max Saidman of CAA Basketball told ESPN. Projected top-20 pick after a strong Combine.
